title: [Proposal] Fix declared-plan skill-scout/progress desync in chat.router step gating

TITLE: Fix declared-plan skill-scout/progress desync in chat.router step gating
PRIORITY: high
TYPE: src_edit
SUMMARY: Patch the declared-plan flow in src/gateway/routes/chat.router.ts so the hidden skill-scout pre-step is tracked independently from visible step 1, preventing repeated false 'run skill_list first' loops after skill_list already succeeded.
FULL IMPLEMENTATION PLAN:
## Why this change
The declared-plan runtime in `src/gateway/routes/chat.router.ts` currently mixes two different concepts into the same progress cursor state:

1. the hidden skill-scout pre-step (`skill_list`, optional `skill_read`), and
2. the visible declared plan step 1.

Today, `declare_plan` is treated like a normal tool-progress event before its special-case handler short-circuits, and `complete_plan_step` still reports success through `markProgressStepResult(true, toolName)` even when it only closed the hidden skill-scout pre-step. That causes the runtime progress tracker, `stepCursor`, and manual skill-scout gate flags to drift out of sync. The user-facing symptom is the loop where:

- `skill_list` already ran successfully,
- `complete_plan_step` reports the scout is done,
- but later rounds still re-block with `"Step 1 is not complete yet: run skill_list first..."` or otherwise mis-handle declared step 1 state.

The fix should make the hidden scout phase a real independent gate instead of an overloaded pseudo-step layered onto visible step 1 progress.

## Exact source edits
File to edit: `src/gateway/routes/chat.router.ts`

### 1) Add explicit skill-scout completion state next to the existing declared-plan gate flags
Around `src/gateway/routes/chat.router.ts:875-879`, where the current state is declared:
- `manualPlanSkillScoutRequired`
- `manualPlanSkillListDone`
- `manualPlanSkillReadCount`

add a new boolean flag dedicated to the hidden pre-step completion state, e.g. `manualPlanSkillScoutCompleted = false`, so the runtime can distinguish:
- scout still required,
- scout already completed for this declared plan,
- visible declared step 1 still pending.

### 2) Reset all scout-state deterministically on every new declare_plan
In the `declare_plan` handler at `src/gateway/routes/chat.router.ts:4137-4167`, keep the existing seeded declared-plan behavior, but make these concrete changes:

- Ensure `declare_plan` fully resets all skill-scout state every time a fresh plan is declared:
  - `manualPlanSkillScoutRequired = plannedSteps.length >= 2`
  - `manualPlanSkillScoutCompleted = false`
  - `manualPlanSkillListDone = false`
  - `manualPlanSkillReadCount = 0`
  - `stepCursor = 0`
  - `consecutiveStepFailures = 0`
- Preserve the existing progress seeding through `seedProgressFromLines(plannedSteps, 'declared', { manualStepAdvance: true })`.
- Update the tool message content so it clearly says the skill-scout is a pre-step and that declared step 1 remains the next visible step after scout completion.

### 3) Stop treating declare_plan as normal step-progress activity
At the main tool loop around `src/gateway/routes/chat.router.ts:4132-4167`, `markProgressStepStart(toolName)` currently runs before the special `declare_plan` branch.

Patch this so `declare_plan` does **not** mutate visible step progress before its custom handler runs. Concretely:
- move the unconditional `markProgressStepStart(toolName)` call so it happens only for normal tools, or
- add a narrow guard so `declare_plan` skips `markProgressStepStart` entirely.

The result after patch must be:
- `declare_plan` seeds the declared plan,
- visible step 1 begins in `pending` / first actionable state from the declared plan seed,
- no synthetic `step_started` / `step_progress` noise is emitted for `declare_plan` itself.

### 4) Make the skill-scout gate key off explicit scout completion, not only skill_listDone
At the declared-plan gate around `src/gateway/routes/chat.router.ts:3764-3799` and the `complete_plan_step` blocker around `4213-4239`, replace the fragile `!manualPlanSkillListDone` logic with explicit scout-phase state:

- While `manualPlanSkillScoutRequired === true` and `manualPlanSkillScoutCompleted === false` on `stepCursor === 0`, only allow scout tools:
  - `skill_list`
  - `skill_read`
  - `request_tool_category`
  - `complete_plan_step`
  - `step_complete`
  - `declare_plan`
- When the model tries to close the step before running `skill_list`, keep blocking with the current style of message.
- After `skill_list` succeeds, do **not** treat visible step 1 as complete yet. Only mark the hidden scout phase as completable.

This means the blocker inside `complete_plan_step` should only fire when the scout phase has not actually been satisfied yet, not merely because flags were reset after scout completion.

### 5) Make complete_plan_step close either the hidden scout pre-step OR the visible plan step, never both implicitly
In the `complete_plan_step / step_complete` handler at `src/gateway/routes/chat.router.ts:4213-4321`, split behavior into two explicit branches:

#### Branch A — completing the hidden skill-scout pre-step
When all are true:
- `progressState.manualStepAdvance`
- `manualPlanSkillScoutRequired`
- `!manualPlanSkillScoutCompleted`
- `stepCursor === 0`
- `manualPlanSkillListDone === true`

then this tool call should:
- set `manualPlanSkillScoutCompleted = true`
- set `manualPlanSkillScoutRequired = false`
- clear only scout-input counters that are no longer needed (`manualPlanSkillListDone = false`, `manualPlanSkillReadCount = 0` if desired)
- **not** call `advanceProgressStep('step_done')`
- **not** advance `stepCursor`
- return a success message like the current one: scout pre-step complete, declared step 1 has not advanced, proceed with step 1 now
- avoid any later generic success handling that would make this look like visible step 1 progress

#### Branch B — completing an actual visible declared step
When the hidden scout phase is already completed (or not required), a `complete_plan_step` call should:
- use the existing task-session persistence behavior if `sessionId.startsWith('task_')`
- call `advanceProgressStep('step_done')` for interactive declared plans
- emit the normal success message

The core invariant after patch:
- one `complete_plan_step` call closes exactly one layer of work
- the scout pre-step closure cannot accidentally progress visible step 1
- the visible step 1 closure cannot be re-blocked by stale scout logic

### 6) Prevent generic markProgressStepResult(true, toolName) from re-classifying scout-pre-step completion as visible-step progress
At the end of the `complete_plan_step / step_complete` branch (`src/gateway/routes/chat.router.ts:4311-4319`), the unconditional:
- `markProgressStepResult(true, toolName)`

must be made conditional.

For scout-pre-step-only completion, do **not** feed that success through the normal manual-plan progress machinery, because that path emits `step_progress` on the cursor step and contributes to the desync.

Concrete patch requirement:
- if the tool call only completed the hidden scout pre-step, skip `markProgressStepResult(true, toolName)` entirely, or replace it with a dedicated no-op / logging path that does not mutate visible step status.
- if the tool call completed a real visible step, keep the existing success progression behavior.

### 7) Keep successful skill_list / skill_read accounting limited to the scout phase
At `src/gateway/routes/chat.router.ts:4541-4548`, keep counting:
- successful `skill_list` => `manualPlanSkillListDone = true`
- successful `skill_read` => increment count

but make the accounting conditional on the scout phase still being active and incomplete. After the scout pre-step is completed, later `skill_list` or `skill_read` calls must not resurrect or interfere with declared step 1 gating.

### 8) Preserve task-session semantics
Do not break the existing task-runner persistence path inside `complete_plan_step / step_complete`:
- `loadTask(taskId)`
- `mutatePlan(... op: 'complete' ...)`
- `setTaskStepRunning(...)`
- `task_step_done` SSE emission

The change must only separate hidden scout completion from visible step advancement. Background task plan persistence should remain deterministic.

## Deterministic behavior after patch
After the patch, declared-plan behavior in `chat.router.ts` must be exactly this:

1. User/model calls `declare_plan([...])`
   - declared steps are seeded into progress UI
   - visible step 1 is pending/in-progress as usual for the declared plan
   - hidden scout phase is marked required and incomplete

2. Model calls `skill_list`
   - scout accounting records that `skill_list` succeeded
   - visible step 1 does **not** advance

3. Optional `skill_read`
   - scout accounting increments read count
   - visible step 1 does **not** advance

4. Model calls `complete_plan_step`
   - if this is still the hidden scout phase, only the scout phase closes
   - response explicitly says visible declared step 1 has not advanced and should begin now
   - future rounds do **not** re-block with `run skill_list first`

5. Model executes actual step-1 work
   - progress remains anchored on visible step 1

6. Model calls `complete_plan_step` again
   - now visible step 1 advances to step 2
   - normal declared-plan progression resumes

No path should allow:
- `declare_plan` to count as visible step work,
- scout completion to implicitly advance visible step 1,
- post-scout rounds to re-open the `run skill_list first` blocker unless a brand-new plan was declared.

## Acceptance tests
1. **Interactive declared plan: scout closes cleanly**
   - Start an interactive session.
   - Call `declare_plan` with at least 2 steps.
   - Call `skill_list` successfully.
   - Call `complete_plan_step`.
   - Expected:
     - success message says scout pre-step completed
     - visible declared step 1 has not advanced yet
     - next non-scout action is allowed without any `run skill_list first` error

2. **Interactive declared plan: early complete_plan_step still blocked**
   - Call `declare_plan` with at least 2 steps.
   - Immediately call `complete_plan_step` before `skill_list`.
   - Expected:
     - blocked with the existing scout guidance
     - step cursor remains on visible step 1
     - no false advancement occurs

3. **Visible step 1 advances only after actual step work + second completion**
   - `declare_plan`
   - `skill_list`
   - `complete_plan_step` (closes scout only)
   - run one or more normal tools for actual step 1 work
   - `complete_plan_step` again
   - Expected:
     - step cursor advances from step 1 to step 2 only on the second completion

4. **Re-declaring a new plan fully resets scout state**
   - Complete scout for one declared plan.
   - Call `declare_plan` again with a new step list.
   - Expected:
     - scout is required again for the new plan
     - prior scout-completed state does not leak into the new plan

5. **Later skill_list calls do not resurrect scout gate**
   - After scout completion for a declared plan, call `skill_list` again later.
   - Expected:
     - no effect on visible step progression
     - no new `run skill_list first` blocker appears

6. **Task session compatibility**
   - In a `task_...` session with a declared plan, complete the scout phase, then complete a real step.
   - Expected:
     - task-store plan mutation and `task_step_done` events still work exactly once per real visible step
     - scout closure alone does not incorrectly mutate task-store step advancement beyond the intended semantics

## Risks and compatibility
- This patch changes core plan/progress behavior in `chat.router.ts`, so the main compatibility risk is unintended regressions in other manual-step flows that share `markProgressStepStart`, `markProgressStepResult`, or `complete_plan_step`.
- The safest implementation is a minimal, local fix: add explicit scout-phase state and gate the generic progress mutations only for the `declare_plan` and scout-pre-step branches.
- The patch should avoid changing unrelated automatic tool-sequence progress logic, browser/desktop continuation logic, or task-store persistence semantics.
- Because the bug is state-machine desync, the main regression risk is off-by-one progress behavior. Acceptance testing should explicitly confirm cursor position before and after scout completion and again after the first real visible step completion.


AFFECTED FILES:
  - [edit] src/gateway/routes/chat.router.ts: Separate hidden skill-scout pre-step state from visible declared-plan step progression and prevent declare_plan/scout completion from mutating normal progress state incorrectly.


DIFF PREVIEW / REFERENCE:
```
chat.router.ts
- add explicit manualPlanSkillScoutCompleted state near manualPlanSkillScoutRequired/manualPlanSkillListDone
- skip generic markProgressStepStart() for declare_plan before its special-case handler
- reset scout state deterministically on every declare_plan
- gate skill-scout blocking on explicit scout completion state, not only manualPlanSkillListDone
- split complete_plan_step into scout-pre-step completion vs real visible-step completion paths
- skip generic markProgressStepResult(true, toolName) when only the hidden scout phase was closed
- only record skill_list/skill_read scout accounting while scout phase is still active
```

EXPECTED IMPACT: Eliminates false repeated 'run skill_list first' loops in declared plans, keeps progress UI and stepCursor aligned, and makes manual plan execution deterministic.

BUILD REQUIRED: Yes — run npm run build after edits. MANDATORY. Stop if build fails — task will pause for assistance.
INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Read affected files with the correct source read tool first (read_source for src/, read_webui_source for web-ui/) to understand current state.
2. Apply edits using the source tool matching the target path and edit operation (src/*_source for src/, webui/*_webui_source for web-ui/).
3. For one-time parallel side work, use additive background primitives: background_spawn, background_status/background_progress, background_join.
4. Run: run_command({command:"npm run build", shell:true}). If it fails, STOP — do not retry.
5. Report exactly what was changed and any issues encountered.
ADDITIONAL EXECUTOR INSTRUCTIONS:
Apply the approved patch exactly in src/gateway/routes/chat.router.ts. Implement the declared-plan state-machine fix described in the proposal: add explicit scout-pre-step completion state, prevent declare_plan from being treated as normal progress activity before its special handler, split complete_plan_step behavior into hidden-scout completion vs real visible-step completion, prevent scout-only completion from flowing through generic markProgressStepResult success logic, and restrict skill_list/skill_read accounting to the active scout phase only. Preserve task-session persistence logic. After editing, run the TypeScript build and report exact changed lines/behaviors verified.
